mnemonics.title: Queue Operations
mnemonics.content: ENQUEUE AT REAR, DEQUEUE FROM FRONT - EAR (Enqueue Rear, Dequeue Front)

mnemonics.title: Queue Properties
mnemonics.content: FIFO - FIRST IN FIRST OUT - Like a ticket line, first to wait gets served first

acronyms.usage: The fundamental principle of queue data structure determining the order of element insertion and removal
Adding an element to the back/end of the queue
Removing an element from the front of the queue

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a glass tube where balls enter from one end and exit from the other. The ball that enters first exits first - just like people in a queue.
Think of front as the 'head' (where service happens) and rear as the 'tail' (where new people join the line). The front is on the LEFT and rear on the RIGHT when visualizing horizontally.
Imagine a round pizza. When you reach the edge, you continue from the opposite side. The array becomes a circle where the end connects back to the beginning.

analogies.analogy: Like a checkout line at a supermarket: customers join at the back (rear) and get served at the front (front). The first person in line gets checkout first.
Like a bus that can hold only 50 passengers. When all 50 seats are filled, no more passengers can board until someone gets off - this is overflow.
Like trying to serve customers when no one is in line - there is no one to serve, which is underflow.
Imagine a round dining table where people can keep coming and going. Empty chairs get filled as people leave, rather than everyone having to shift - this is how circular queue reuses space.
